mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity and higher corporate earnings, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month changes in INDPRO do not exhibit a positive rank correlation with 20-day S&P 500 forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% restricts credit availability and raises discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, the S&P 500 posts positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in the unemployment rate and consumer prices signal stagflationary pressure that erodes profit margins and compresses valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Months where both UNRATE and CPIAUCSL increase do not show significantly negative 60-day forward returns compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, a month-over-month decline in retail sales forecasts demand contraction and downward earnings revisions, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines do not produce negative 5-day forward returns during quarters when real GDP is in its top quartile of the cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicate growing wage income and consumer spending capacity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month changes in PAYEMS show no positive rank correlation with 20-day S&P 500 forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation accelerating faster than 0.5% month-over-month triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and lower real disposable income, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with CPIAUCSL increasing more than 0.5% do not exhibit negative 5-day forward returns in at least 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated federal funds rates and falling retail sales compresses corporate revenue and raises debt-service costs, jointly driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS level and RSAFS month-over-month change fails to significantly predict negative 20-day returns (t-stat < 2.0).

mechanism_narrative: In post-recession recovery periods, accelerating industrial production signals rebounding capacity utilization and profit margin expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases following a GDP trough do not generate positive 60-day forward returns with a hit rate above 55%.
